title: Platform Site Promotion + Rollback (dev → staging → prod with release tags)
label: site-promotion
purpose: Formalize the promotion ladder for FAMtastic sites so dev/staging/main branches map cleanly to local/Netlify-staging/Netlify-prod, and so any production deploy can be rolled back to a prior release tag in one command.
why_now: MBSH canonicalization on 2026-05-05 surfaced that the promote/rollback flow exists in convention only — branches exist on every site but no command formalizes the transitions, no release tags are written, and no rollback path is wired. Without this, a bad prod deploy has no clean revert.

origin.summary: User asked to confirm site repos are isolated from Studio history, and to design promote-to-prod + rollback procedures with release tags or branches.

success_signal: Running `platform site promote mbsh-reunion --to prod` performs: merge staging→main, write release tag (date-based), push, wait for Netlify prod deploy, write proof. `platform site rollback mbsh-reunion` reverts main to the previous tag. Both work end-to-end against MBSH and at least one other site.
non_goals: Replacing Netlify's deploy mechanism (Netlify still does the actual deploy on push).
Automating DNS changes for new domains (handled by separate platform/dns/* capabilities).

workstreams.title: platform site promote <tag> --to <staging|prod>
outputs: platform/capabilities/site/promote.sh
Merges current branch → target branch (dev→staging or staging→main)
On --to prod: writes annotated release tag using date-based scheme (vYYYY.MM.DD-HHMM)
Pushes branch + tag to origin (single Netlify project handles both via branch deploys + production branch)
Polls Netlify deploy status until success/failure (uses Netlify Deploys API filtered by branch)
Writes proof to proofs/site-promote-<tag>-<env>-<date>.json
Refuses promotion if working tree is dirty or tests are failing (configurable)
acceptance: platform site promote mbsh-reunion --to staging merges dev→staging, pushes, Netlify branch-deploys to <tag>--staging.netlify.app.
platform site promote mbsh-reunion --to prod merges staging→main, writes tag, pushes, Netlify production-deploys to custom domain.
Proof file records: source branch, target branch, merge commit SHA, tag, Netlify deploy id + URL, deploy status.

workstreams.title: platform site rollback <tag> [--to <tag-name>]
outputs: platform/capabilities/site/rollback.sh
Default behavior: revert main to the previous release tag
--to <tag-name>: revert main to a specific named tag
Implementation: git checkout <tag> -- frontend/ backend/ config/ → commit on main with revert message → push
Writes proof to proofs/site-rollback-<tag>-<date>.json with from/to tag SHAs
acceptance: Rollback reverts site to a named or implicit prior tag.
Rollback never deletes git history; it always creates a new revert commit.
Netlify auto-redeploys on the new commit and serves the prior version.

workstreams.title: Release tag scheme — date-based for sites
outputs: Tag format: v<YYYY>.<MM>.<DD>-<HHMM> (UTC)
Tag message captures: site tag, branch, commit SHA, deployer (vault user), Netlify deploy id
Documented in docs/operating-rules/site-release-tags.md
acceptance: Every prod deploy writes exactly one tag.
Tags are pushable, listable, and addressable by rollback.

workstreams.title: ONE Netlify project per site with branch deploys
workstreams.description: Replaces the earlier 'two Netlify projects per site' design. One Netlify project per site is simpler and matches how Netlify is built to be used. Branch deploys handle staging; production branch + custom domain handle prod.
outputs: Convention: ONE Netlify project per site, named <tag>
Production branch: main → serves custom domain (e.g. mbsh96reunion.com)
Branch deploys enabled for: staging → serves <tag>--staging.netlify.app
Branch deploys disabled for: dev (local only, no Netlify build)
platform/capabilities/deploy/connect-netlify.sh updated to configure this single-project model
Documented in docs/operating-rules/site-netlify-convention.md
acceptance: Running connect-netlify creates ONE Netlify project linked to the site GitHub repo.
Pushing to staging triggers a branch deploy at <tag>--staging.netlify.app.
Pushing to main triggers a production deploy at <custom-domain> (or <tag>.netlify.app pre-domain).
Existing sites can be retroactively brought into compliance via a --reconcile flag.
MBSH is the first site reconciled to this model.

workstreams.title: Assisted-Manual Capability Pattern (open vendor UI + auto-detect completion)
workstreams.description: Generalizes the 'this step requires the vendor UI' problem. Instead of leaving the user with a copy-paste URL, Site Studio renders an Open <Vendor> button that launches the exact deep-link URL, then polls the vendor API until the manual step is detected as complete, then auto-advances. Applies to Netlify git-link, Resend domain verify, GoDaddy DNS confirmation, Stripe webhook setup, etc.
outputs: Pattern doc: docs/operating-rules/assisted-manual-capability.md
Shared UI component: site-studio/public/js/assisted-manual-step.js — renders [Open <Vendor> →] button + status pill + polling indicator
Shared CSS: site-studio/public/css/assisted-manual-step.css
Per-vendor adapters: site-studio/lib/assisted-manual/{netlify,resend,godaddy}.js — each exports {deepLink(ctx), poll(ctx) → 'pending'|'done'|'failed'}
First applied to Netlify git-link in connect-netlify.sh
acceptance: Clicking [Open Netlify →] opens the exact site-deploys-settings URL in a new tab (window.open with _blank).
Studio polls the vendor API every 5s, surfaces a status pill ('waiting for repo link…' → '✓ linked').
On detection, Studio auto-runs the next dependent step (e.g., trigger first staging deploy) and writes a proof.
Pattern is reused for at least one non-Netlify vendor in the same plan cycle to prove generalizability.

workstreams.title: Promote/rollback proof capture (Ops integration)
outputs: Each promote/rollback writes a proof packet to proofs/ with type='site-promotion' or 'site-rollback'
Cross-link to plan and to the Ops Reviews tab (when Ops workspace ships)

workstreams.title: Governance gates on prod promotion
outputs: Promotion to prod requires governance approval token matching plans/registry.json governance.hard_stop_conditions for production_deploy
Rollback bypasses the gate when --reason 'incident' is supplied (faster recovery), but writes a louder proof
acceptance: Prod promote without token is refused.
Rollback with --reason incident proceeds and writes proof flagged 'emergency'.

scope: ws_promote_command working for staging and prod
ws_rollback_command working for default-prev-tag and named-tag
ws_netlify_single_project_branch_deploys wired for MBSH (one project, branch deploys for staging, production branch + custom domain for prod)
ws_assisted_manual_capability — pattern shipped, used for the one-time Netlify git-link step
Custom domain (mbsh96reunion.com) bound to main-branch deploys via the assisted-manual flow for GoDaddy DNS
explicit_non_mvp: Multi-site reconcile flag (one site is enough to prove the pattern)

known_gaps_opened: Netlify ↔ GitHub linking still requires the vendor UI (Netlify API limitation). Mitigated by the assisted-manual capability pattern (ws_assisted_manual_capability) — Studio opens the right URL and polls for completion.
platform/capabilities/site/promote.sh and rollback.sh do not exist yet.
No assisted-manual UI components exist yet (site-studio/public/js/assisted-manual-step.js + adapters).
Existing Netlify project mbsh-reunion-staging was renamed but lost its repo link in the API rename — must be re-linked via UI as the first proof of the assisted-manual pattern.

design_corrections.from: Two Netlify projects per site (<tag>-staging and <tag>)
design_corrections.to: ONE Netlify project per site with branch deploys (staging branch → branch deploy URL, main branch → production + custom domain)
design_corrections.why: Simpler, native to how Netlify works, fewer projects to manage. User caught the over-engineering during plan review.
